summary: The V2 batch lands the intended contract shape without overbuilding: explicit invocation modes are validated, future adapters have a conformance target, Codex Cloud and Symphony recipes share a proof-return contract, and tests/validators pass.

finding_log.summary: Codex Cloud command vocabulary is version-sensitive.
evidence: `codex cloud --help` and subcommand help were checked locally on 2026-05-06.
The recipe explicitly says to refresh help output if the local CLI changes.
finding_log.next_action: Refresh the help-output note whenever changing the Codex Cloud recipe.

finding_log.summary: A future agent may confuse `mode: external_runner` with `computeTarget: codex_cloud`.
evidence: The recipe states `computeTarget: codex_cloud` remains blocked locally.
The cloud prompt uses `mode: external_runner` and `computeTarget: local_shared` because the cloud workspace is already chosen.
finding_log.next_action: Keep this distinction in future adapter tickets; add adapter code only when a real cloud submission owner exists.

finding_log.summary: Comment-trigger support remains a convention, not an implementation.
evidence: The docs repeatedly state comments must be converted by an external caller into a `CodexterRunEnvelope`.
No listener, webhook, polling, or external board client code was added.
finding_log.next_action: Future Linear/Notion/GitHub work must start from the conformance scaffold and explicitly own comment-to-envelope conversion.

rubric_sections.name: code-quality
findings: The only production-code change is `MODES` validation in the existing diagnostic helper.
New tests cover external-runner envelopes, invalid daemon-like modes, filesystem adapter conformance, and Codex Cloud unsupported-target blocking.
rubric_sections.next_action: No code follow-up required for this batch.

rubric_sections.name: evidence-quality
findings: Focused unit tests, py_compile, metadata, doc parity, harness invariant, helper prepare, Codex Cloud help, and guardrail grep checks are recorded.
The evidence artifact maps the checks to the main AI misunderstanding risks.
rubric_sections.next_action: Archive the completed tickets after final metadata and diff checks pass.

rubric_sections.name: integration-readiness
findings: Docs now agree that Codexter owns invocation/proof contracts while Symphony and Codex Cloud own external execution lifecycles.
No new external clients, watchers, daemons, cloud submission wrappers, or auto-apply paths were introduced.
rubric_sections.next_action: Stage only the V2 batch files; leave unrelated frontend/media dirty work untouched.

rubric_sections.name: user-intent-satisfaction
findings: The result prioritizes the user's actual Codexter story: not replacing Symphony, but making Codexter easy for normal Codex, Codex Cloud, or Symphony to invoke later.
The review explicitly lists AI misunderstanding risks and keeps the future work capped.
rubric_sections.next_action: Return to real project work unless a concrete adapter/runtime project ticket appears.

risks_and_uncertainties_for_future_agent: Codex Cloud CLI subcommands may drift; refresh local help before editing the recipe.
A future agent may try to implement comment listening because `ticket_comment` exists. The current contract only defines the convention.
A future agent may set `computeTarget: codex_cloud` in local runs and expect it to launch cloud compute. It intentionally blocks until an adapter exists.
A future Symphony worker should use `mode: symphony_worker` and usually `computeTarget: local_shared`, not `computeTarget: symphony`, after Symphony has already chosen the workspace.
Board adapter conformance is a scaffold, not permission to add Linear/Notion/GitHub credentials or network calls.
Codex Cloud `apply` must stay after diff inspection, local tests, review, and proof verification.
next_action: No V2 follow-up required; return to real project work unless a concrete adapter or runtime ticket appears.
